connectors.description: Connects to the Adobe Acrobat Sign REST v6 API. Creates, sends, reminds, cancels, and downloads e-signature agreements; manages library templates, users, workflows, and webhook subscriptions. Auto-discovers the caller's shard (na1, eu1, jp1, etc.) via /baseUris after OAuth code exchange.

install_fields.help: From Adobe Developer Console → Adobe Sign integration → Client ID. Required for OAuth code exchange.
install_fields.label: Adobe Sign Client ID
install_fields.placeholder: Paste your Adobe Sign OAuth client ID

install_fields.help: From Adobe Developer Console → Adobe Sign integration → Client Secret. Never logged. Required for OAuth code exchange.
install_fields.label: Adobe Sign Client Secret
install_fields.placeholder: Paste your Adobe Sign OAuth client secret

install_fields.help: Adobe Sign OAuth host. Most accounts use the NA1 host even when their data lives on EU1 / JP1 / etc. — the per-shard API origin is discovered automatically after token exchange via /baseUris.
install_fields.label: OAuth Host

install_fields.default: user_read user_write agreement_read agreement_write agreement_send library_read library_write workflow_read
install_fields.help: Adobe Sign OAuth scopes. The integration in Developer Console must be configured with at least the same scopes — Adobe rejects any scope not registered there.
install_fields.label: OAuth Scopes

install_fields.help: Per-request httpx timeout for Adobe Sign API calls. Default 60s.
install_fields.label: Request timeout (seconds)

install_fields.help: OAuth2 Client ID from ADP Marketplace → My Apps → your app credentials.

install_fields.help: Inline PEM cert chain ADP issued when you registered the consumer application. mTLS is mandatory — every TLS handshake (including the token mint) must present this certificate.

install_fields.help: Inline PEM private key paired with the certificate above. Stored encrypted; written to a tmp file at install time so httpx can load it through `cert=(crt, key)`.

install_fields.help: Base URL for the ADP REST API. Leave blank to use the default.

apis.description: Validates app_id + api_key by probing `GET /1/indexes`, then persists config.
apis.name: Install Connector

apis.description: Probes `GET /1/indexes` (DSN-routed read host) as a lightweight liveness check.
apis.name: Health Check

apis.description: GET /1/indexes — list every index on the Algolia application. Returns `{items, nbPages}`.
apis.name: List Indexes

apis.description: PUT /1/indexes/{name}/settings — replace all index settings. Algolia creates the index on the first settings push.
apis.name: Create / Replace Index Settings

apis.description: GET /1/indexes/{name}/settings — fetch the full settings object for an index.
apis.name: Get Index Settings

apis.description: Upsert a single object. With `object_id` → `PUT /1/indexes/{name}/{id}` (overwrite). Without → `POST /1/indexes/{name}` (Algolia generates ID).
apis.name: Save Object

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/{name}/batch — bulk indexing. `action` one of addObject | updateObject | partialUpdateObject | partialUpdateObjectNoCreate | deleteObject.
apis.name: Save Objects (Batch)

apis.description: GET /1/indexes/{name}/{id} — fetch a single object. Optional `attributes` projection.
apis.name: Get Object

apis.description: DELETE /1/indexes/{name}/{id} — delete a single object.
apis.name: Delete Object

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/{name}/{id}/partial — patch specific attributes. `create_if_not_exists` controls upsert vs strict-update semantics.
apis.name: Partial Update Object

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/{name}/browse — cursor-paginated full-index export. Pass `cursor` from each response to fetch the next page.
apis.name: Browse Index

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/{name}/query — keyword search with optional filters + pagination.
apis.name: Search Index

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/*/queries — federated search across multiple indexes in one round-trip. Each request is `{indexName, query, ...}`.
apis.name: Multi-Index Search

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/{name}/synonyms/search — paginated synonym search with optional type filter.
apis.name: List Synonyms

apis.description: PUT /1/indexes/{name}/synonyms/{id} — upsert a synonym record. `forward_to_replicas` propagates to replica indexes.
apis.name: Save Synonym

apis.description: POST /1/indexes/{name}/rules/search — paginated search for merchandising rules.
apis.name: List Rules

apis.description: PUT /1/indexes/{name}/rules/{id} — upsert a merchandising rule (condition + consequence).
apis.name: Save Rule

connectors.description: Connects to Algolia (search-as-a-service) to manage indexes, push and query objects, run keyword and federated searches, and curate synonyms + merchandising rules. Uses Algolia's official DSN-routed read host (`<app_id>-dsn.algolia.net`) and write host (`<app_id>.algolia.net`) with `algolianet.com` fallbacks for high availability.

install_fields.help: Found in Algolia Dashboard → API Keys → Application ID. A short uppercase alphanumeric string (e.g. 'LATENCY'). Sent on every request as the `X-Algolia-Application-Id` header.
install_fields.label: Application ID

install_fields.help: Algolia Admin API Key (or a sufficiently-scoped key). Found in Algolia Dashboard → API Keys → Admin API Key. Treat as secret — Admin keys grant full read/write across the entire application. Sent on every request as the `X-Algolia-API-Key` header.
install_fields.label: API Key

install_fields.help: Optional default index name. Surfaced to the dashboard as a hint — the connector does not auto-route to this index.
install_fields.label: Default Index

install_fields.help: Per-request httpx timeout. The connector retries on transient failures, so this is a single-attempt cap.
install_fields.label: Request Timeout (seconds)

connectors.name: AlgoliaConnector

apis.description: Validates access_key_id and secret_access_key are present and stores config. Does not call AWS.
apis.name: Install Connector

apis.description: Verifies credentials by calling ListBuckets (or HeadBucket on default_bucket when configured). Returns CONNECTED on success, EXPIRED on AccessDenied/InvalidAccessKeyId.
apis.name: Health Check

apis.description: Returns all buckets the credentials can see, as [{name, creation_date}].
apis.name: List Buckets

apis.description: Creates a new bucket. The region defaults to the connector's configured region; pass 'region' to override. Honours the us-east-1 LocationConstraint quirk.
apis.name: Create Bucket

apis.description: Deletes a bucket. WARNING: the bucket must be empty — AWS rejects DeleteBucket on a non-empty bucket. Empty it first via list_objects + delete_object.
apis.name: Delete Bucket

apis.description: Existence + access probe against a single bucket. Returns {bucket, exists: True} on 2xx; raises AwsS3NotFound on 404.
apis.name: Head Bucket

apis.description: Lists objects in a bucket under an optional key prefix. Paginated via continuation_token.
apis.name: List Objects

apis.description: ListObjectVersions — for versioned buckets. Returns both Versions and DeleteMarkers.
apis.name: List Object Versions

apis.description: HeadObject — returns size, etag, content_type, last_modified, user metadata, version_id.
apis.name: Get Object Metadata

apis.description: Fetches an object and returns its full body as bytes. For large objects, prefer generate_presigned_url.
apis.name: Get Object (bytes)

apis.description: Uploads bytes to bucket/key. Sets ContentType and optional user metadata (ASCII-coerced).
apis.name: Put Object

apis.description: Deletes a single object. Idempotent on AWS — succeeds even if the key does not exist.
apis.name: Delete Object

apis.description: Server-side copy of source_bucket/source_key to dest_bucket/dest_key. No body transit through the connector.
apis.name: Copy Object

apis.description: PutObjectAcl with a canned ACL string (private, public-read, public-read-write, authenticated-read, bucket-owner-read, bucket-owner-full-control).
apis.name: Set Object ACL

apis.description: Returns a time-limited URL for direct browser access. operation defaults to 'get_object'; pass 'put_object' for upload URLs. AWS caps SigV4 GET URLs at 7 days.
apis.name: Generate Presigned URL

connectors.description: Connects to Amazon S3 (and S3-compatible providers such as Cloudflare R2, Wasabi, Backblaze B2, MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces) to list, read, write, copy, and delete objects, generate presigned URLs, manage object ACLs and bucket versions, and create/delete buckets. Uses aiobotocore + AWS Signature V4 — never rolls its own signing.

install_fields.help: 20-character access key ID issued by AWS IAM. Found in IAM Console → Users → Security credentials → Access keys.
install_fields.label: AWS Access Key ID

install_fields.help: 40-character secret access key paired with the access key ID. Stored encrypted; shown only once at creation time in AWS.
install_fields.label: AWS Secret Access Key

install_fields.help: AWS region code (e.g. us-east-1, eu-west-1, ap-south-1). Defaults to us-east-1. For S3-compatible providers, use whatever region label they document or 'auto'.
install_fields.label: AWS Region

install_fields.help: Override the S3 endpoint for non-AWS providers: Cloudflare R2, Wasabi, Backblaze B2, MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces. Leave blank to talk to AWS S3.
install_fields.label: Endpoint URL Override (S3-compatible)

install_fields.help: Optional. Required when using temporary credentials from AWS STS, AssumeRole, or federated identity. Leave blank for long-lived IAM access keys.
install_fields.label: STS Session Token

install_fields.help: Optional convenience: pre-populate the bucket name in the UI for object-level actions. When set, health checks call HeadBucket against this bucket — useful for IAM policies scoped to a single bucket without ListAllMyBuckets.
install_fields.label: Default Bucket

connectors.description: Connects to the Azure DevOps Services REST API (Projects, Teams, Users, Repos, Pull Requests, Work Items via WIQL, Builds, Pipelines, Releases) using Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication. Auth is HTTP Basic with an empty username + the PAT as the password. Every request carries the api-version query parameter.

install_fields.help: Your Azure DevOps organization slug — the {org} segment of https://dev.azure.com/{org}.
install_fields.label: Organization

install_fields.help: Create at User Settings → Personal Access Tokens. Scope the PAT to the surfaces you need (Code, Work Items, Build, Release). The PAT is sent HTTP-Basic-encoded with an empty username — never as a Bearer token.
install_fields.label: Personal Access Token

install_fields.help: Azure DevOps REST API version. Sent as ?api-version=<value> on every request. Leave blank to use 7.1.

install_fields.help: Default project name used by sync() and by methods that omit the project argument.
install_fields.label: Default Project

install_fields.help: Maximum Azure DevOps API requests per minute. Default is 200.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

Uses session-based authentication seeded by a 4-piece credential bundle (userName + password + orgId + devKey) and transparently re-logs-in when Bill.com rejects a stale sessionId.

connectors.description: Connects to the Bitbucket Cloud REST API (Workspaces, Repositories, Branches, Pull Requests, Issues, Commits, Webhooks, raw file content) using OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant. Supports list / get / create / merge across PRs and issues, repository CRUD, and KB sync of open pull requests.

Per-tenant cluster URL — every tenant brings their own base_url.

connectors.description: Connects to a tenant-hosted n8n workflow-automation instance (Cloud or self-hosted) via the public REST API at {instance_url}/api/v1. Supports workflow CRUD + activation + transfer, execution listing / inspection / deletion, credential management, tags, instance users and variables. Sync ingests workflows + executions as NormalizedDocuments. Auth = API key via X-N8N-API-KEY header.

install_fields.help: The Odoo user login — typically an email address. Must belong to a user with permission on the models you intend to read or write.

install_fields.help: Generate from Odoo → Preferences → Account Security → API Keys. Use an API key, never your account password — Odoo blocks JSON-RPC logins that present a password when 2FA is enabled.

install_fields.placeholder: Paste the API key generated in Odoo

install_fields.help: Maximum requests per minute the connector will issue. Odoo has no hard public rate-limit; the default keeps traffic gentle on shared instances.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

connectors.description: Connects to the Pinecone vector database. Wraps the control plane (https://api.pinecone.io — indexes, collections) and per-index data plane (vectors upsert/query/fetch/update/delete, stats, namespaces) behind a single API-key install. Auto-caches the index→host map after the first describe_index call.

install_fields.help: From Pinecone Console → API Keys. The key is sent RAW in the `Api-Key` header (NOT Authorization) on every control- and data-plane request.

install_fields.help: Pinecone environment / region tag for the project (e.g. `us-east-1-aws`, `gcp-starter`). Used as a fallback when building legacy data-plane hosts.
install_fields.label: Environment

install_fields.help: Optional Pinecone project ID. Used as a fallback to build `https://{index}-{project_id}.svc.{environment}.pinecone.io` when the control plane does not return a host.
install_fields.label: Project ID

install_fields.help: Index name used when an API call omits index_name. Leave blank to require it on every call.
install_fields.label: Default Index

install_fields.help: Namespace used when an API call omits namespace. Empty string targets the default namespace.
install_fields.label: Default Namespace

install_fields.help: Pinecone control-plane base URL for index/collection CRUD. Leave blank to use the default.
install_fields.label: Control Plane Base URL

install_fields.help: Value sent as X-Pinecone-API-Version. Pin to a known-good date for stability.
install_fields.label: API Version

install_fields.help: Soft cap on outbound requests per minute. Pinecone's serverless plan rate limit is 100/min by default.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

install_fields.help: From PostHog → Personal Settings → Personal API Keys. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` on every management call (projects, persons, flags, cohorts, insights, queries, dashboards, etc.). Shown once at generation — rotate if lost.

install_fields.help: From Project Settings → Project API Key. Required ONLY for capture surfaces (capture_event, batch_capture, identify_person, alias_distinct_ids). Sent inside the JSON body as `api_key` — never as a header. This is the same key shipped in posthog-js client bundles.
install_fields.label: Project API Key

install_fields.help: https://app.posthog.com (default), https://us.posthog.com (US Cloud explicit), https://eu.posthog.com (EU Cloud), or your self-hosted PostHog URL.

install_fields.help: Client-side soft cap. PostHog management API default is 240/min per personal key. Capture endpoints are much higher.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

Server-scoped surfaces use the server_token (X-Postmark-Server-Token); account-scoped surfaces (Servers, Domains) use the account_token (X-Postmark-Account-Token). The connector picks the right header per call.

connectors.description: Connects to SugarCRM (REST API v11) to read, search, create, update, and delete Contacts, Accounts, Opportunities, Leads, and Meetings via OAuth2. Supports both the password grant (on-prem) and the authorization-code grant (SugarCloud).

install_fields.help: e.g. https://yourcompany.sugarondemand.com — the root URL of your SugarCRM deployment. The connector appends /rest/v11 automatically.
install_fields.label: SugarCRM Site URL

install_fields.help: OAuth2 client ID. Defaults to 'sugar' (SugarCRM's built-in trusted client for the password grant).
install_fields.label: OAuth2 Client ID

install_fields.help: OAuth2 client secret. Leave blank when using the default 'sugar' client; required for custom OAuth applications.
install_fields.label: OAuth2 Client Secret
install_fields.placeholder: Leave blank for the default 'sugar' client

install_fields.help: Required when grant_type=password. Use a SugarCRM service account with the minimum scopes you intend to expose.
install_fields.label: SugarCRM Username

install_fields.help: Required when grant_type=password. Stored encrypted by Shielva.
install_fields.label: SugarCRM Password

install_fields.help: 'password' for on-prem deployments, 'authorization_code' for SugarCloud / SugarCRM OAuth applications.
install_fields.label: OAuth2 Grant Type

install_fields.help: SugarCRM 'platform' string sent with every token request. Use 'api' for service-account integrations.
install_fields.label: Platform Identifier

install_fields.help: Required only when grant_type=authorization_code. Must match the redirect URI registered on the SugarCRM OAuth client exactly.
install_fields.label: OAuth2 Redirect URI

install_fields.help: Maximum SugarCRM API requests per minute. Default 60 is conservative; raise to match your SugarCRM license tier.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

apis.description: Validates bot_token by probing /getMe, then (optionally) registers the configured webhook_url via setWebhook.
apis.name: Install Connector

apis.description: API-key flow has no OAuth exchange. Returns a TokenInfo wrapping the configured bot_token for SDK ABI compatibility.
apis.name: Authorize

apis.description: Verifies Telegram API connectivity by calling /getMe.
apis.name: Health Check

apis.description: Drains pending updates from getUpdates into the Shielva knowledge base, advancing the last_update_id checkpoint.
apis.name: Sync Updates

apis.description: Returns basic information about the bot — id, username, name, capability flags.
apis.name: Get Bot Identity

apis.description: Sends a text message to a chat. Supports HTML / MarkdownV2 / Markdown parse modes and inline keyboards via reply_markup.
apis.name: Send Message

apis.description: Edits the text of an existing message. Returns the edited Message object.
apis.name: Edit Message Text

apis.description: Deletes a message. Bots can delete their own messages and messages in groups where they have the right permissions.
apis.name: Delete Message

apis.description: Sends a photo by URL with an optional caption.
apis.name: Send Photo

apis.description: Sends a file by URL with an optional caption.
apis.name: Send Document

apis.description: Forwards a message from one chat to another.
apis.name: Forward Message

apis.description: Long-polls or short-polls the Telegram Bot API for new updates. Use offset to acknowledge previously-received updates and timeout > 0 to enable long polling.
apis.name: Get Updates

apis.description: Registers an HTTPS endpoint to receive updates. Optional secret_token is echoed back in the X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header so the receiver can authenticate the source.
apis.name: Set Webhook

apis.description: Unregisters the webhook so the bot returns to short-polling mode.
apis.name: Delete Webhook

apis.description: Returns the current webhook URL, last error, pending update count, and allowed_updates filter.
apis.name: Get Webhook Info

apis.description: Responds to a callback query from an inline keyboard. Optionally shows an alert.
apis.name: Answer Callback Query

apis.description: Returns up-to-date information about a chat (private, group, supergroup, channel).
apis.name: Get Chat

apis.description: Returns information about a single member of a chat — status, role, custom title.
apis.name: Get Chat Member

apis.description: Lists administrators of a group / supergroup / channel.
apis.name: Get Chat Administrators

apis.description: Resolves a file_id to a downloadable file_path. Combine with http_client.file_url(token, file_path) to build the actual download URL.
apis.name: Get File

apis.description: Routes an inbound Telegram webhook POST through process_callback (X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token verification) and dispatches the populated update subfield to handle_event.
apis.name: Handle Webhook

apis.description: Constant-time compares the X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header against the configured webhook_secret_token. Returns {verified: bool, data?: dict, error?: str}.
apis.name: Process Callback

apis.description: Dispatches one Telegram update kind (message / callback_query / inline_query / …) to its specialised _handle_<kind> method.
apis.name: Handle Event

apis.description: Processes a batch of queued Telegram updates one-at-a-time, swallowing per-item errors so a single malformed update never poisons the batch.
apis.name: Batch Processor

connectors.description: Connects to the Telegram Bot API to send/edit/delete/forward messages, send media, manage webhooks, long-poll updates, inspect chats and admins, and resolve file_ids. Authentication is a bot token embedded in the request URL path (no Authorization header).

install_fields.help: Get from @BotFather on Telegram — format like 123456:ABC-DEF... The token is sent as a URL path segment (/bot{token}/{method}); there is no Authorization header.
install_fields.label: Bot Token

install_fields.help: Optional HTTPS endpoint Telegram will POST updates to. When set, install() registers it via setWebhook.
install_fields.label: Webhook URL

install_fields.help: Optional secret that Telegram echoes back in the X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header on every webhook POST. process_callback() verifies it with constant-time compare.
install_fields.label: Webhook Secret Token

install_fields.help: Base URL for the Telegram Bot API. Override for a self-hosted Bot API server. Leave blank to use the default.
install_fields.label: Telegram API Base URL

install_fields.help: Default formatting for outgoing message text. Supported values: HTML, MarkdownV2, Markdown.
install_fields.label: Default Parse Mode

install_fields.help: Client-side soft cap. Telegram's documented global limit is roughly 30 messages/second across all chats.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

apis.description: Validates subdomain + api_key and probes /users.json before marking the connector installed.
apis.name: Install Connector

apis.description: Verifies Wufoo API connectivity by fetching the list of users.
apis.name: Health Check

apis.description: GET /users.json — returns every user the API key can see (used as the health probe).
apis.name: List Users

apis.description: GET /forms.json — returns summary metadata for every form on the account.
apis.name: List Forms

apis.description: GET /forms/{form_id}.json — returns a single form's metadata by ID or hash.
apis.name: Get Form

apis.description: GET /forms/{form_id}/fields.json — returns the field definitions (id, type, required, sub-fields) for a form.
apis.name: List Fields

apis.description: GET /forms/{form_id}/entries.json — paginated, filterable, sortable entries for a form. Filter list is forwarded as Filter1, Filter2, … with match=AND.
apis.name: List Entries

apis.description: Resolves a single entry by EntryId via the filtered entries endpoint (Wufoo v3 has no canonical /entries/{id} route).
apis.name: Get Entry

apis.description: POST /forms/{form_id}/entries.json — submits a new entry. field_values must be keyed by Wufoo field IDs (Field1, Field2, …); sent as application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
apis.name: Create Entry

apis.description: GET /forms/{form_id}/entries/count.json — returns the total number of entries for a form.
apis.name: Count Entries

apis.description: DELETE /forms/{form_id}/entries/{entry_id}.json — removes a single entry from a form.
apis.name: Delete Entry

apis.description: GET /forms/{form_id}/comments.json — paginated comments attached to a form's entries.
apis.name: List Comments

apis.description: POST /forms/{form_id}/comments.json — attaches a comment to an entry on a form.
apis.name: Add Comment

apis.description: GET /reports.json — returns summary metadata for every report on the account.
apis.name: List Reports

apis.description: GET /reports/{report_id}.json — returns a single report by ID or hash.
apis.name: Get Report

apis.description: GET /reports/{report_id}/widgets.json — returns the widgets that make up a report (charts, tallies, etc.).
apis.name: List Report Widgets

apis.description: GET /forms/{form_id}/webhooks.json — returns every webhook registered against a form.
apis.name: List Webhooks

apis.description: PUT /forms/{form_id}/webhooks.json — registers a new webhook URL on a form. Optional handshake_key signs incoming payloads; metadata=true sends the form field schema with each delivery.
apis.name: Create Webhook

apis.description: DELETE /forms/{form_id}/webhooks/{webhook_hash}.json — unregisters a previously-registered webhook.
apis.name: Delete Webhook

connectors.description: Connects to the Wufoo REST API (v3) — Forms, Entries, Fields, Reports, Users, Webhooks, Comments — using an account API key over HTTP Basic. The base URL is subdomain-specific: https://{subdomain}.wufoo.com/api/v3.

install_fields.help: Your Wufoo subdomain — if your dashboard URL is acme.wufoo.com, enter 'acme'. The connector constructs https://{subdomain}.wufoo.com/api/v3 from this value.
install_fields.label: Wufoo Subdomain

install_fields.help: From Wufoo → Account → API Information. Sent as the HTTP Basic auth username; the password is the literal placeholder string 'footastic' (any non-empty string is accepted by Wufoo).
install_fields.label: Wufoo API Key

install_fields.help: Soft cap on Wufoo REST requests per minute. Default 60 matches Wufoo's standard quota.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)

install_fields.help: Override the default subdomain-derived base URL. Leave blank for production; useful for proxies and integration tests.
install_fields.label: Wufoo API Base URL

connectors.description: Connects to the JetBrains YouTrack REST API (Issues, Projects, Users, Boards, Sprints, Articles, Comments, Tags, Time Tracking) using a permanent token. Supports issue CRUD, comments, agile boards, knowledge-base articles, and KB sync of issues.

install_fields.help: Per-tenant YouTrack instance URL. Cloud: https://{instance}.youtrack.cloud. Self-hosted: https://youtrack.example.com. The connector appends /api automatically.
install_fields.label: YouTrack Base URL

install_fields.help: Generate at YouTrack -> Profile -> Account Security -> Permanent Tokens. Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <perm:...>`. Tokens start with `perm:`.

install_fields.help: Optional. If set, sync() filters by this project and create_issue uses it as a fallback when no project_id is supplied.
install_fields.label: Default Project ID

install_fields.help: Soft client-side cap on requests/minute against the YouTrack API.
install_fields.label: Rate Limit (requests/min)
